title: Prism Breaks — Movement I
prompt: Instrumental only. First movement of a continuous 10-minute futuristic broken-beat focus session at 104 BPM. Preserve the elegant sonic world of the original Prism Breaks study: a syncopated kick-and-rim pattern with a light two-step feel, clipped hand percussion, a rubbery but restrained sub-bass motif, airy synthetic mallet plucks, and an expansive cool pad. Shape a calm long-form arc rather than a loop: 0:00–0:25 a soft signal arrives; 0:25–2:30 a clean, stable broken groove; 2:30–4:25 a subtle bass and mallet variation; 4:25–5:24 a slightly wider luminous harmonic color. Keep each change sparse and patient, with enough low-frequency articulation for a contained visual response. In the final six seconds, gently thin upper percussion and leave bass and pad breathing for a seamless handoff. No vocals, no lo-fi, no Rhodes, no acoustic piano, no vinyl dust, no jazzy chords, no trap hats, no dramatic build-up, no drop, no heavy wobble bass, no harsh treble, no EDM risers, and no abrupt ending.
commercialUseNote: Verify the active ElevenLabs paid-plan music terms before commercial publication.
